title: Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur
tagline: Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Soundareswararswamy Temple or Sountharyanathar temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the God Shiva.
overview.description: The Soundareswararswamy Temple or Sountharyanathar temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the God Shiva. It is situated in the village of Thiruppanaiyur, three and a half kilometres (2.2 mi) from Nannilam. The temple is centred on a palmyra tree which is the sthalavrksham. The palmyra tree gives its name to the village itself ("Thiru" (holy) + "Panai" (palm) + "Ur" (city) or the "city of the holy palm").

topPlaces.name: Adhalaiyur
topPlaces.description: Adhalaiyur is a village in Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu State of India.

topPlaces.name: Alangudi, Nannilam
topPlaces.description: Alangudi is a village in the Nannilam taluk of Tiruvarur district in Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Sannanallur
topPlaces.description: Sannanallur is a area in Nannilam Town in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu and part of Nannilam Taluk. Until the early 1990s, the village was part of Thanjavur district, prior to it being split into three new districts.

topPlaces.name: Enangudi
topPlaces.description: Enangudi is the main village which is unified with neighbouring villages like Adalaiyur, Karipakkam, Ketharimangalam, Putthagaram, Kayathoor, Karuppuoor, Thepiramangalam, Vawvaladi and Pakkam Kottor. The nearest town of significant size is Tiruvarur which is 16 km away, Mayiladuthurai is 30 km and Nagapattinam is around 28 km away.

topPlaces.name: Thirukondeeswaram Pasupatheeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Thirukondeeswaram Pasupatheeswarar Temple (Tamil: திருக்கொண்டீஸ்வரம் பசுபதீஸ்வரர் கோயில்) is a Hindu temple located at Thirukondeeswaram in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. The temple is dedicated to Shiva, as the moolavar presiding deity, in his manifestation as Pasupatheeswarar.

topPlaces.name: Veerateeswarar Temple, Thiruvirkudi
topPlaces.description: Veerateeswarar Temple (also called Thiruvirkudi Veerateeswarar temple) is a Hindu temple located at Thiruvirkudi in Mayiladuthurai district of Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Shiva in the form of Veerateswarar and his consort is known as Elavar Kuzhali.

topPlaces.name: Nannilam block
topPlaces.description: Nannilam block is a revenue block in the Nannilam taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 48 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple
topPlaces.description: Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple is a Hindu temple located in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India, dedicated to Lord Rama, the seventh avatar of Lord Vishnu. The temple is glorified by Maharishi Valmiki and classified as one of the 108 Abhimana Kshethrams of the Vaishnavate tradition.

items.note: Adhalaiyur is a village in Nagapattinam district in Tamil Nadu State of India.
Alangudi is a village in the Nannilam taluk of Tiruvarur district in Tamil Nadu, India.

Sannanallur is a area in Nannilam Town in Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu and part of Nannilam Taluk. Until the early 1990s, the village was part of Thanjavur district, prior to it being split into three new districts.

Enangudi is the main village which is unified with neighbouring villages like Adalaiyur, Karipakkam, Ketharimangalam, Putthagaram, Kayathoor, Karuppuoor, Thepiramangalam, Vawvaladi and Pakkam Kottor. The nearest town of significant size is Tiruvarur which is 16 km away, Mayiladuthurai is 30 km and Nagapattinam is around 28 km away.

Veerateeswarar Temple (also called Thiruvirkudi Veerateeswarar temple) is a Hindu temple located at Thiruvirkudi in Mayiladuthurai district of Tamil Nadu, India. The presiding deity is Shiva in the form of Veerateswarar and his consort is known as Elavar Kuzhali.
Nannilam block is a revenue block in the Nannilam taluk of Tiruvarur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 48 panchayat villages.

Mudikondan Kothandaramar Temple is a Hindu temple located in the Tiruvarur district of Tamil Nadu, India, dedicated to Lord Rama, the seventh avatar of Lord Vishnu. The temple is glorified by Maharishi Valmiki and classified as one of the 108 Abhimana Kshethrams of the Vaishnavate tradition.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur?
faq.a: The best time to visit Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (104 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (106 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur?
faq.a: Stays in Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Soundareswararswamy Temple, Thiruppanaiyur?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adhalaiyur, Alangudi, Nannilam, Sannanallur, Enangudi, Thirukondeeswaram Pasupatheeswarar Temple.
